_field_documentation.id: Unique kebab-case slug for this sample
_field_documentation.category: Subdir name: capcut_desktop | capcut_mobile | jianying_legacy | modern_encrypted
_field_documentation.app_version: Semantic version string from the app's `new_version` field (e.g. '5.0.0'); null if unknown
_field_documentation.schema_version: Internal `version` integer from the draft JSON; null if unknown or encrypted
_field_documentation.timeline_entry_path: Path *relative to this manifest* pointing at the file to feed inspect/convert. Real-world samples often live outside the repo for privacy — in that case use source_app_path and leave this null.
_field_documentation.source_app_path: (optional) Original location on the contributor's machine; redact usernames as needed
_field_documentation.added: YYYY-MM-DD when this sample was added
_field_documentation.notes: Free-form: what was edited in this draft, why it's interesting, any quirks
_field_documentation.verified_against: (optional) cutsmith version+commit confirmed to convert this sample cleanly
_field_documentation.shape: (optional) Quick structural summary for triage: tracks, clips, durations, features exercised

_categories.jianying_legacy: Older 剪映 PC builds that still emit plaintext draft_content.json
_categories.modern_encrypted: Modern 剪映 PC (≥ 75.0.0) with encrypted Timelines/<UUID>/draft_info.json — kept for triage only, v0.1 does not parse these

samples.shape: 1 video track (1 clip @ 151.4s), 1 audio track (1 clip), 154 text materials on 2 text tracks, 0 effects/transitions
samples.notes: Long-form talking-head workflow: 25-minute source MP4 sliced once (141.4s → 292.9s); heavy captioning but no multi-cut editing. Validates: modern_plaintext schema reads, sequence name fallback (project dir 0509 vs file stem draft_info), text materials go to unsupported. Limitation: only one cut, so doesn't exercise multi-clip alignment.

samples.shape: 2 video tracks (V1: 7 clips butt-cut, V2: 1 overlay), 2 audio tracks (BGM 60s + 1 SFX hit), 0 unsupported items after v0.1.1 P1 fixes
samples.notes: Multi-cut + overlay sample. Validates: V1 continuity across 7 cuts to 60.000s, V2 overlay placement, BGM/SFX dual audio tracks, no double-audio, all extra_material_refs benign (canvases/sound_channel_mappings/1.0× speeds). Was the trigger for v0.1.1 P1 fixes (speed false-positive tolerance, extra_refs categorization).

samples.shape: 2 video tracks (V1: 7 clips + V2: 2 incl. 0.640× speed-changed clip), 2 audio tracks, 1 text track, 1 sticker track, 1 filter track, 15 unsupported (text 3, sticker 3, transition 2, effect, video_effect, hsl, loudness, filter, speed, speed_curve)
samples.notes: Stress test for v0.1.1 alpha: captions + stickers + effects + filters + transitions + variable speed all in one draft. Validates: P3 fix (V2.1 speed clip occupies target_dur=13.800s, not source_dur=8.833s, subsequent clips don't drift), P1 extra_refs categorization (every unsupported item has a specific category — no blanket extra_refs), name fallback (cutsmith2 not draft_info). Premiere import requires manual verification by user; the report's Time Remapping suggestion applies to V2.1 specifically.
